message.content:
Investigate the 500 errors that occur when loading the dashboard at https://nexus-ai-silk-six.vercel.app/dashboard after login.

Use Playwright to:
1. Go to https://nexus-ai-silk-six.vercel.app/login
2. Login with email "testuser@nexusai-test.com" and password "TestPassword123!"
3. Wait for redirect to /dashboard
4. Capture ALL network requests and responses, especially any that return 500 status codes
5. Log the full URL, status code, and response body of any failed requests (4xx or 5xx)
6. Also check the browser console for any error messages

Save all findings to C:\Users\User\nexus-ai\test-screenshots\debug-500.txt

Be very thorough - capture every failing request URL and its response body.

content.text: I'll investigate the 500 errors on the dashboard by creating a Playwright script to capture all network requests, responses, and console errors. Let me start by understanding the project structure and setting up the test.

input.thought:
Let me analyze the root cause of the 500 errors.

The error message is: `{"code":"42P17","details":null,"hint":null,"message":"infinite recursion detected in policy for relation \"profiles\""}`

PostgreSQL error code 42P17 means "infinite recursion detected in policy". 

Looking at the RLS policies on the `profiles` table:

1. `"Users can view own profile"` - `FOR SELECT USING (id = auth.uid())` -- This is fine, no recursion.

2. `"Users can update own profile"` - `FOR UPDATE USING (id = auth.uid())` -- Fine too.

3. `"Users can view org members"` - `FOR SELECT USING (organization_id IN (SELECT organization_id FROM public.profiles WHERE id = auth.uid()))` -- THIS IS THE PROBLEM!

Policy #3 does a SELECT on `public.profiles` within the USING clause of a policy ON `public.profiles`. When PostgreSQL evaluates this policy, it needs to check the inner SELECT which itself requires evaluating the RLS policies on `profiles` again, causing infinite recursion.

The `is_org_member` function is defined as `SECURITY DEFINER` which means it bypasses RLS. But the policy #3 directly queries `profiles` from within a policy on `profiles`.

The fix would be to either:
- Use `auth.uid()` directly instead of querying profiles within the profiles policy
- Make the inner query bypass RLS by using a SECURITY DEFINER function
- Remove the recursive policy and rely on the simpler policy #1

The `use-user.ts` hook does:
```js
const { data } = await supabase
  .from('profiles')
  .select('*')
  .eq('id', user.id)
  .single();
```

This triggers the SELECT policies on profiles. Policy #1 would work fine (id = auth.uid()), but policy #3 causes the recursion because PostgreSQL evaluates ALL applicable policies.

The fix is to replace policy #3 with one that uses a SECURITY DEFINER function to get the user's org_id without recursion.

################################################################################
################################################################################
##                                                                            ##
##                    ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS & DIAGNOSIS                         ##
##                                                                            ##
################################################################################
################################################################################

================================================================================
1. PRIMARY ISSUE: Supabase RLS Infinite Recursion on "profiles" Table (HTTP 500)
================================================================================

FAILING REQUEST:
  Method:  GET
  URL:     https://yoaxcxlnnkrezwwkfphs.supabase.co/rest/v1/profiles?select=*&id=eq.e0b51dd3-3a13-4a3a-84c7-c4d94bcb4aa8
  Status:  500
  Error:   {"code":"42P17","details":null,"hint":null,"message":"infinite recursion detected in policy for relation \"profiles\""}

This error occurs EVERY TIME the dashboard loads. It was triggered 5+ times across
the test session. The user profile fails to load, which means the Topbar component
cannot display the user's name/avatar.

TRIGGERED BY (in client code):
  File: src/hooks/use-user.ts (line 20-24)
  Code:
    const { data } = await supabase
      .from('profiles')
      .select('*')
      .eq('id', user.id)
      .single();

  This is called by the <Topbar /> component (src/components/layout/topbar.tsx)
  via the useUser() hook on EVERY dashboard page load.

ROOT CAUSE (in database):
  File: supabase/migrations/20260225000000_initial_schema.sql (lines 228-233)

  The third RLS policy on the profiles table causes infinite recursion:

    CREATE POLICY "Users can view org members" ON public.profiles
      FOR SELECT USING (
        organization_id IN (
          SELECT organization_id FROM public.profiles WHERE id = auth.uid()
        )
      );

  PostgreSQL evaluates ALL applicable SELECT policies on the profiles table.
  When this policy runs, its USING clause does a sub-SELECT on public.profiles,
  which triggers the same RLS policies again, which triggers the sub-SELECT again,
  creating an infinite loop.

  The first two policies are fine:
    - "Users can view own profile":  FOR SELECT USING (id = auth.uid())        -- OK
    - "Users can update own profile": FOR UPDATE USING (id = auth.uid())       -- OK

  But the third policy references the same table it's protecting:
    - "Users can view org members":  FOR SELECT USING (... FROM public.profiles ...) -- RECURSIVE!

FIX OPTIONS:

  Option A (Recommended): Replace the recursive policy with a SECURITY DEFINER function
  that bypasses RLS when looking up the user's organization_id:

    -- Create a helper function that runs with elevated privileges
    CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_user_org_id()
    RETURNS UUID AS $$
      SELECT organization_id FROM public.profiles WHERE id = auth.uid()
    $$ LANGUAGE sql SECURITY DEFINER STABLE;

    -- Drop the recursive policy
    DROP POLICY "Users can view org members" ON public.profiles;

    -- Recreate it using the helper function (which bypasses RLS)
    CREATE POLICY "Users can view org members" ON public.profiles
      FOR SELECT USING (
        organization_id = public.get_user_org_id()
      );

  Option B: Simply remove the "Users can view org members" policy entirely if
  users only need to see their own profile (the first policy handles that).

  Option C: Use auth.uid() directly and store org info differently to avoid
  the self-referencing query.


================================================================================
2. SECONDARY ISSUE: 404 on /dashboard/templates/new (HTTP 404)
================================================================================

FAILING REQUEST:
  Method:  GET
  URL:     https://nexus-ai-silk-six.vercel.app/dashboard/templates/new?_rsc=1grla
  Status:  404

  This appears to be a prefetch for a route that doesn't exist yet.
  The /dashboard/templates page loads fine (200), but Next.js RSC prefetching
  tries to fetch /dashboard/templates/new which has no corresponding page.tsx.
  This is a minor issue - the route simply hasn't been implemented yet.


================================================================================
3. MINOR ISSUE: /api/documents/upload Returns 405 (Method Not Allowed)
================================================================================

  This is expected behavior - the upload endpoint only accepts POST requests,
  and the test hit it with GET. Not a real bug.


================================================================================
4. net::ERR_ABORTED Requests (Not Real Failures)
================================================================================

  Multiple RSC prefetch requests show as ERR_ABORTED. These are Next.js React
  Server Components (RSC) prefetch requests that get cancelled when the user
  navigates away before they complete. This is normal Next.js behavior and
  does NOT indicate actual errors.


================================================================================
5. IMPACT ASSESSMENT
================================================================================

  - The profiles 500 error occurs on EVERY page that uses the <Topbar /> component
    (which is ALL dashboard pages via the dashboard layout)
  - The user profile data (name, avatar) will not load in the top-right dropdown
  - The auth state change listener in use-user.ts will also trigger the same 500
    on every auth event (login, token refresh, etc.)
  - The dashboard page itself still renders (200 status) because the page content
    is static and doesn't query the profiles table
  - The Topbar gracefully degrades: it shows "?" as avatar initials and "User"
    as the name when profile data is null


================================================================================
6. SUMMARY OF ALL 500 ERRORS
================================================================================

  All 500 errors originate from the SAME root cause:

  URL: https://yoaxcxlnnkrezwwkfphs.supabase.co/rest/v1/profiles?select=*&id=eq.<user-id>
  Error Code: 42P17 (PostgreSQL infinite recursion in policy)
  Error Message: "infinite recursion detected in policy for relation \"profiles\""
  Occurrences during test: 5+
  Affected policy: "Users can view org members" on public.profiles

  FIX: Replace the self-referencing RLS policy with a SECURITY DEFINER
  helper function to break the recursion cycle.

content.text:
All findings have been saved. Here is the complete investigation summary:

---

## Investigation Results

All findings are saved to `C:\Users\User\nexus-ai\test-screenshots\debug-500.txt` (1015 lines, 61KB), along with 6 screenshots.

### Primary Finding: Infinite Recursion in Supabase RLS Policy (HTTP 500)

**Every single 500 error traces back to one root cause** -- a self-referencing Row Level Security (RLS) policy on the `profiles` table in Supabase.

**Failing request** (triggered 5+ times during a single dashboard load):
```
GET https://yoaxcxlnnkrezwwkfphs.supabase.co/rest/v1/profiles?select=*&id=eq.e0b51dd3-3a13-4a3a-84c7-c4d94bcb4aa8
Status: 500
Response: {"code":"42P17","details":null,"hint":null,"message":"infinite recursion detected in policy for relation \"profiles\""}
```

**The problematic policy** is at line 228-233 of `C:\Users\User\nexus-ai\supabase\migrations\20260225000000_initial_schema.sql`:

```sql
CREATE POLICY "Users can view org members" ON public.profiles
  FOR SELECT USING (
    organization_id IN (
      SELECT organization_id FROM public.profiles WHERE id = auth.uid()
    )
  );
```

This policy does a `SELECT` on `public.profiles` within a policy that is itself ON `public.profiles`. When PostgreSQL evaluates the outer SELECT, it must evaluate this policy, which requires a sub-SELECT on profiles, which requires evaluating this same policy again, creating an infinite recursion loop.

**The trigger in application code** is `C:\Users\User\nexus-ai\src\hooks\use-user.ts` (lines 20-24), which queries the profiles table via the Supabase client. This hook is used by the `<Topbar />` component (`C:\Users\User\nexus-ai\src\components\layout\topbar.tsx`), which is rendered on every dashboard page via the dashboard layout (`C:\Users\User\nexus-ai\src\app\(dashboard)\layout.tsx`).

### Recommended Fix

Replace the recursive policy with a `SECURITY DEFINER` helper function that bypasses RLS:

```sql
-- Create a helper function that runs with elevated privileges (bypasses RLS)
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_user_org_id()
RETURNS UUID AS $$
  SELECT organization_id FROM public.profiles WHERE id = auth.uid()
$$ LANGUAGE sql SECURITY DEFINER STABLE;

-- Drop the recursive policy
DROP POLICY "Users can view org members" ON public.profiles;

-- Recreate using the helper function
CREATE POLICY "Users can view org members" ON public.profiles
  FOR SELECT USING (
    organization_id = public.get_user_org_id()
  );
```

### Secondary Issues Found

1. **404 on `/dashboard/templates/new`** -- Next.js RSC prefetching tries to load a route that has no `page.tsx` implementation. Minor issue; the route simply hasn't been built yet.

2. **`net::ERR_ABORTED` on various RSC requests** -- These are normal Next.js React Server Component prefetch cancellations when navigating between pages. Not actual errors.

3. **405 on `/api/documents/upload`** -- Expected; this endpoint only accepts POST, not GET.

### Impact

- The dashboard page itself still renders (HTTP 200) because the page content is static
- The user profile (name, avatar) in the top-right user menu fails to load on every page
- The `<Topbar />` gracefully degrades, showing "?" as initials and "User" as the display name
- The error fires repeatedly due to the `onAuthStateChange` listener in `use-user.ts`
